chapter_context.summary: A warning against seductive philosophies and legalistic regulations. Paul declares that in Christ dwells all the fullness of God, and believers have been 'circumcised with the circumcision made without hands.'

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Steadfastness in Christ (2:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Colossians.
This section focuses on Freedom from Human Philosophy (2:8-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Colossians.
This section focuses on Freedom from Legalism (2:16-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Colossians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Section Context: Steadfastness in Christ**
This verse in Colossians 2 reinforces the central theme of Christ's sufficiency and the believer's new identity in Him.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry reflects on the 'fullness of Christ' and how it meets every need of the soul as seen in this passage.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the linguistic precision Paul uses to combat the early Gnostic tendencies in Colossae.

key_themes.title: The Supremacy of Christ
key_themes.description: Christ as the Creator, Sustainer, and Head of the Church.

key_themes.title: Completeness in Christ
key_themes.description: Believers need nothing else for salvation or spiritual maturity besides Christ.

key_themes.title: The New Man
key_themes.description: Putting off the old self and putting on the character of Christ.

devotional.question: What does it mean for you to be 'complete in Him' (Col 2:10)?

cultural_historical.title: The Colossian Heresy
cultural_historical.text: The church in Colossae was facing a mix of Jewish legalism, Greek philosophy, and mystic asceticism, which Paul addresses by pointing back to Christ.
